elements.text: Art of Prompting - Key Elements

content.text:
WHAT
do I want?

WHY / WHO
do I need it?

WHERE
to look?

HOW
do I need it?

Goal (Objective)
What response do you want from Copilot?
(Desired outcome)

Context, Persona &
Audience
Why do you need it?
Who is involved?
Act as a <Persona>

Source (Grounding)
Which information
sources should Copilot
use (ground against)?

Expectations
How should Copilot
respond to meet your
expectations?

elements.text: How to use: Copilot in Word in the document

content.text: Note the specific prompts shown may vary

elements.text: Sample use cases

content.text: Turn an outline or idea into text
Rewrite existing text for length, tone, new phrasing
Add tables based on the text

elements.text: What can you do in the document

content.text: **Draft with Copilot** – Add content to the Word doc
Add suggested image (next to the title)
Put information into a table using Visualize as a table
Provide ideas or reference a file
Provide ideas, reference a file, or let Copilot write
Rewrite or add a table
Add an image
Summary of the Value Guide

elements.alt: A composite image showing the Copilot interface in Word, including a text box for drafting, buttons for 'Reference a file' and 'Inspire me', and a menu with options 'Rewrite' and 'Visualize as a table'.

elements.text: How to use: Copilot in Word in the Copilot chat pane

content.text: Note the specific prompts shown may vary
Sample use cases
Summarize or answer questions about an existing document
Use the Copilot chat pane to
Summarize
Command (Ask)
Select a predefined prompt in the Copilot sidebar. You can then add more context.
Ask general knowledge questions or ask for creative ideas. You'll need to review any content for factual mistakes

elements.alt: Microsoft Word logo
Microsoft Copilot logo
Screenshot of Copilot in Word interface showing a chat pane with draft and prompt options.

elements.text: Practice : Create a Copilot training plan

content.text: Go to Copilot in Teams, Word or to
In chat box write this example prompt below
"Create a training plan to help me with adopting M365 co-pilot. Include a list of daily activities that I can do that help me to build Copilot into my daily routine"
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Practice : From Copilot Chat to Word

content.text: Go to a long (preferably complicated) Word document and open Copilot
Enter the following prompt:
You can also use Copilot in Word to summarize, add content, expand or rewrite particular sections.
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Demo

elements.alt: The Microsoft Loop logo, consisting of a stylized, multi-colored infinity-like loop shape.

elements.text: Creating a new Document with Copilot in Word

elements.alt: Microsoft logo in the top-left corner

elements.text: Practice : Creating a new Word document with Copilot in Word

content.text: Go to Word or

Enter the following prompt: "Write a new document on the cost, benefits, and considerations for installing solar panels on homes. Provide an introduction at the beginning of the document and a summary at the end of the document. Include a table of contents"
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.alt: Screenshot of the Microsoft Word interface with the Copilot prompt input box visible in the center.

elements.text: Practice : Creating a new Word document with Copilot in Word

content.text: Go to Word or https://www.microsoft365.com/launch/word and create a new Blank Document
Enter the following prompt: "Give me a list of questions to ask an agency about a new policy" or you may enter your own prompt: "Write a new document about <this topic> and include <these details>."
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.alt: A screenshot of the Microsoft Word online interface showing a blank document with the 'Draft with Copilot' prompt box.

elements.text: Practice : Use Existing Documents to Create a new Word document with Copilot in Word

content.text: Go to Word or
Enter a prompt using the forward / to reference existing content:

“Draft a response to the Tailwind RFP using the /<RFP reply framework> to format the response. Reference the /<Boulder Internal Meeting> and the /<Commercial Charger Pro Spec> for technical details and pricing. Include discount details from the following email /<Exciting Opportunity> ”
This is a sample prompt that is using 4 different reference documents.
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Modifying an existing document with Copilot in Word

elements.alt: Microsoft logo in the top left corner.

elements.text: Practice : Modifying an existing Word document with Copilot in Word

content.text: In an existing Word document (or one used for this practice)
Select and highlight an existing sentence or a paragraph.
Click the Copilot icon on the left of the new line
Enter the following prompt: "Rewrite this sentence, but make it sound more professional."
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Practice : Modifying an existing Word document with Copilot in Word

content.text: In an existing Word document (or one used for this practice)
Navigate to the end of the document
In new line click the Copilot icon on the left of the new line
Click Inspire Me or Keep Writing This
You can regenerate the response more than one time, then you can select the response that you like
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.alt: A screenshot of the Microsoft Word interface showing a document titled 'Questions for Client's Social Media Campaign'. A Copilot assistant is open in the document and has generated a list of questions.

elements.text: Practice : Visualize Content as a Table

content.text: Go to Word or https://www.microsoft365.com/launch/word and create a new Blank Document
Enter the following prompt: "Write a paragraph about the different types of apples."
Select Keep It and Select the target content
Click on the Copilot Icon to the left of the text and select the "Visualize as a Table" option
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.alt: Screenshot of a Microsoft Word document containing a business proposal template, with the Copilot pane open.

elements.text: Analyzing an existing document with Copilot in Word
Practice : Summarize a Document

content.text: Open an existing Word file that you have access to.
At the top of the **file**, click on the **Copilot Icon** in the ribbon menu and open the Copilot pane on the right side of the document.
Click or enter the prompt: **Summarize the document**
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.alt: Screenshot of a Microsoft Word document showing a business proposal with the Copilot pane open on the right side. The Copilot icon is highlighted in the ribbon menu.

elements.text: Practice : Ask Questions about the Document

content.text: Open an existing Word file that you have access to.
At the top of the file, click on the Copilot Icon in the ribbon menu and open the Copilot pane on the right side of the document.
Example Prompt: "What does this document say about <pick a topic>?"
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Transforming a document to an email with Copilot in Word

elements.alt: Microsoft logo in the top left corner

elements.text: Practice : Transform a Document into an Email

content.text: Open an existing Word file that you have access to.
At the top of the file, click on the Copilot Icon in the ribbon menu and open the Copilot pane on the right side of the document.
Enter the Prompt: "Transform this document into an email"
Copilot will create an email draft based on the document content which can be used to send an email
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.alt: A screenshot of Microsoft Word displaying a Business Proposal document with the Copilot pane open on the right side.

elements.text: Find more Word prompts to try in Copilot Lab

content.text: Write an introduction
Summarize this doc
List pros and cons
Add a paragraph
Write an intro paragraph for this document and make it sound [professional]
Summarize this document [in 3key points]
List the pros and cons of [the different project ideas mentioned in this document]
Add a paragraph that captures [the argument of this document]
Change the font
Create an overview
Outline a business plan
Take notes in a meeting
Change the font to [Segoe UI, 12point]
Create a high-level overview of [agile product development]
Draft a business plan outline for a [sustainable marketing company]
Draft a one-page template that I can use to take notes for a meeting. Include sections for the date and topic
Understand quickly
Brainstorm team building ideas
Improve this document
Write more confidently
Explain this document in three sentences
Give me ideas for icebreaker activities for a new team
Give me specific examples from this document on how I can improve it for [a leadership review?]
How can I more concisely describe [time management]?

elements.text: Questions
